Home › Visa Checker › Colombian passport
Passport guide · Colombian

Colombian passport: 119 visa-free destinations

Holders of a Colombia passport have easy entry to 119 countries — 85 visa-free and 34 with visa on arrival. Below is the full, sourced list plus e-visa and visa-required countries.

85visa-free
34visa on arrival
37e-visa
42visa required

Visa-free destinations

Countries a Colombian passport holder can enter without arranging any visa in advance. Day-limit shown where available.

  • Albania
    Visa-free · 90 days
  • Andorra
    Visa-free · 90 days
  • Antigua and Barbuda
    Visa-free

Visa on arrival

Visa issued at the border — typically quick, fee-paid, and no embassy visit required. Still bring passport photos, proof of onward travel, and hotel bookings.

  • Bahrain
    Visa on arrival
  • Bangladesh
    Visa on arrival
  • Burkina Faso
    Visa on arrival
  • Burundi
    Visa on arrival

    Visa on arrival

e-Visa destinations

Apply online before you travel. Usually approved within a few days, cheaper and faster than an embassy visa.

    e-Visa

Visa required — 42 destinations

embassy visa needed · click to expand

These destinations require a full visa from an embassy or consulate in advance. Entries marked ✓ have been re-verified against an official government source.

  • Afghanistan
    Visa required
  • Algeria
    Visa required
  • Angola
    Visa required

Expand your access with a residence permit

If your Colombian passport has limited reach, a residence permit can unlock many additional destinations. Pairing your passport with one of the permits below typically adds 5–15 new visa-free or visa-on-arrival countries:

  • UAE residence permit — strong for South Asian passport holders, unlocks Armenia, Georgia, Azerbaijan, Serbia
  • US Green Card — unlocks Mexico, Canada (via eTA), several Caribbean and Central American countries
  • Schengen residence permit — unlocks Turkey, Balkans, several visa-free short stays across the EU

Frequently asked questions

How many countries can Colombian passport holders visit visa-free?
Colombian passport holders have visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to approximately 119 countries — 85 visa-free and 34 visa-on-arrival. Notable visa-free destinations include Albania, Andorra, Antigua and Barbuda, Argentina, Austria.
Is the Colombian passport strong?
Yes — the Colombian passport is considered strong, giving access to 119+ destinations without needing to arrange a visa in advance.
Do I need an ETA or e-visa for some countries?
Yes. 37 destinations require an e-visa (online application before arrival) for Colombian passport holders. These are typically quicker than embassy visas but still need to be arranged before travel.
Which countries require a full visa in advance?
42 countries require Colombian passport holders to obtain a visa from an embassy before travelling. Check the destination embassy's website for specific requirements and processing times.
Can a residence permit or second visa unlock extra countries?
Yes — several residence permits grant bonus visa-free access regardless of your Colombian passport. See our guides for UAE residence, US Green Card, and Schengen residence permit to learn which countries unlock for each.
How accurate is this list?
Entries marked with a ✓ badge have been re-verified against an official government source (embassy, ministry, or visa portal) and link to that source. Entries without the badge come from the community-maintained open-source Passport Index Dataset — a reasonable starting point but not individually verified. Rules change frequently. Always confirm with the destination embassy before booking travel.
